reforms.description: The Swedish government proposed a shift in the balance of support from university-based institutions to co-financed science parks, aiming to enhance technology diffusion to SMEs and stimulate the development of economically viable innovations in a business-oriented environment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing technology diffusion and supporting innovation in SMEs is expected to raise productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in funding allocation but does not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Technology diffusion, arguably more important for enterprise and job creation than basic R&D, might benefit from transferring public R&D spending from universities to jointly funded science parks.

reforms.description: Sweden has introduced a substantial increase in the capacity of the formal education system, adding 100,000 places in adult education and 30,000 in tertiary education, alongside a reform obliging local authorities to provide three years of upper-secondary schooling for all pupils, aimed at enhancing the skills of the workforce and improving educational attainment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing educational capacity and improving skills is expected to enhance human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major structural change in the education system.
reforms.source_quote: A substantial increase in the capacity of the formal education system, by 100,000 places in adult education aimed at low-skilled workers and 30,000 places in tertiary education.

reforms.description: The Swedish government has initiated a reassessment of the balance between employment protection and enterprise adaptability, alongside proposals to simplify the tax code and government support schemes for small and medium-sized enterprises, and measures to promote research and innovation among small firms through the 'Regional Development Programmes' and venture capital initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating enterprise adaptability and promoting innovation among SMEs is expected to enhance economic growth and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of support mechanisms for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Given a favourable overall cost level and a low corporate taxation, small and medium-sized enterprises may be best served by simplifying both the tax code and the wide array of government support schemes.

reforms.description: The Swedish government has established various funds to support private research, focusing on industrial research institutes that receive significant financing from both government and industry, with the Board for the Development of Industry and Technology (NUTEK) serving as the main channel for government funding of R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting private research is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding mechanisms are currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of funds represents a significant but not transformative change in funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: Various government funds have been created to support private research, the National Board of Technical Development being created in 1968 to promote and co-ordinate the Swedish research effort.
